cit.basis: Flat 22% corporate income tax (Law 4172/2013 Income Tax Code, as amended) on profits of Greek tax-resident legal entities.
cit.credit_institutions_note: Credit institutions that have opted into the deferred-tax-asset (DTA) framework remain taxed at 29% instead of the standard 22%.

business_tax.note: The business tax (telos epitidevmatos) is abolished for individuals and self-employed professionals as of 2025 and is being phased out for legal entities. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: Standard VAT (FPA) 24% with reduced rates of 13% and 6%; 30% reduced rates apply on certain Aegean islands (e.g. Leros, Lesvos, Kos, Samos, Chios). As an EU member state, Greece participates in the One Stop Shop (OSS) for intra-EU B2C supplies.

pillar_two.note: Greece transposed the EU Pillar Two directive via Law 5100/2024 (Council Directive (EU) 2022/2523): a 15% minimum effective rate applies to in-scope groups with consolidated revenue of at least EUR 750 million. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Advance tax payments, WHT on dividends/interest/royalties, tonnage tax for shipping, loss relief, and treaty rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
